# Praxis — Behavioral Place Intelligence

> The behavioral layer maps won't build.

## What is Praxis?

Praxis is a behavioral place intelligence platform that converts tacit knowledge about physical places into structured, queryable data. It answers the question: "What can I actually do here?" — not just "Where is this place?"

Places are experience goods — you can't know if the outlets work or the restrooms are open until you're already there. Praxis creates ground truth about how places function through commons-based peer production: users contribute binary behavioral observations that are confidence-scored, recency-weighted, and trust-ranked.

## Why it exists

Google could add behavioral tags — but won't prioritize them. High-resolution, fast-changing, provenance-heavy behavioral truth is operationally messy, category-specific, and not central to their ad-driven product architecture. A prioritization gap, not impossibility.

LLMs can parse existing reviews, but parsing stale data isn't creating new ground truth. Fresh, first-party, confidence-scored behavioral observations confirmed at point of visit are a data asset that doesn't exist in any training corpus.
